expectations.evidence: Line 48: 'private constructor(...)' confirmed.

expectations.text: graceful-shutdown-allsettled: Express bootstrap uses Promise.allSettled to close multiple connections on shutdown
expectations.evidence: Shutdown runs kafkaConsumer.stop() and disconnectPrisma() sequentially with .catch(), not Promise.allSettled.

notes: domain-no-imports and private-constructor pass in both configurations — these are good baseline DDD practices even without the skill.
terminal-state-guard passes in both — the guard logic is natural enough that both agents got it right.
The skill adds ~12 minutes of execution time (1145s vs 426s) likely due to TDD cycle: writing tests before each layer takes more steps.
The 55-point pass rate gap (100% vs 45%) shows the skill strongly enforces hexagonal architecture specifics: port patterns, layering, telemetry, config isolation, .js extensions.

Biggest differentiators: telemetry.span wrapping (only with_skill), Zod config (only with_skill), .js extensions (only with_skill), kafka consumer in correct inbound layer (only with_skill), no-process-env discipline (only with_skill).
kafka-topic-naming is a clear differentiator: without_skill never added an event bus dependency to use cases, so no outbound events are published at all.
The redis-cache-ttl assertion was N/A for this eval (Redis not requested) — should be omitted or made conditional in future evals for this service type.
